message:
feat(landing): add Get Started & CTA section component

Implements bead f8vs.8 - final section of landing page epic:
- One-command installation block with copy button
- Install options (--easy-mode, --verify, --system)
- Three user paths: Researchers, Developers, Curious
- Quick start preview (4-step CLI workflow)
- Social proof badges (MIT, 4300+ tests, 100% open source)
- Final CTA with Get Started Now button
- Footer note about open source commitment

Includes 10 passing tests covering all sections.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.5","title":"Feature: Discovery & Intelligence Section","description":"# Discovery & Intelligence Section

## Purpose
Show the "smart" features: finding similar hypotheses, exploring what-if scenarios, and tracking robustness. These features differentiate Brenner Lab from simple chat interfaces.

## Key Messages
1. Connect to your past research automatically
2. Explore evidence impact before running tests
3. Know which hypotheses are robust vs fragile
4. Surface duplicates and clusters

## Content Structure

### Section Title
"Intelligence Built In: Search, Simulate, Score"

### Feature Blocks

#### Hypothesis Similarity Search
**"Find Related Work Instantly"**
- Hash-based embeddings work offline
- No API calls, no usage limits
- Surfaces related hypotheses across sessions
- Identifies potential duplicates

Visual: Search interface showing:
- Query hypothesis
- Similar matches with scores
- Breakdown: statement (0.8), mechanism (0.6), domain (0.9)

Key benefits:
- Don't re-investigate killed hypotheses
- Find forgotten related work
- Identify clusters of research questions

Technical callout: "Works entirely client-side - your hypotheses never leave your machine"

#### What-If Scenarios
**"Simulate Before You Experiment"**
- See how test outcomes would affect confidence
- Compare tests by expected information gain
- Prioritize high-impact experiments

Visual: Scenario builder showing:
- Starting confidence: 60%
- If test supports: → 78%
- If test challenges: → 35%
- Expected information gain: 0.42

"Best next test" recommendation with reasoning

#### Robustness Scoring
**"Know Which Hypotheses Are Battle-Tested"**
- Evidence-weighted survival scores
- Separate scores for supporting vs challenging evidence
- Visual robustness indicator (fragile → robust)

Visual: Hypothesis card with robustness meter:
- H1: "Morphogen gradient" - Robustness: 0.72 (High)
  - 3 supporting tests, 1 challenging (survived)
- H2: "Timing mechanism" - Robustness: 0.35 (Low)
  - 1 supporting test, 2 inconclusive

#### Anomaly Detection
**"Quarantine the Weird, Don't Hide It"**
- Anomalies are tracked, not swept under the rug
- Explicit status: active, resolved, deferred, paradigm-shifting
- Can spawn new hypotheses (paradox grounding)

Visual: Anomaly register with:
- X-001: "Oscillating fate markers" - Status: Active
- "Conflicts with H1, H2 - neither model predicts oscillation"
- "Spawned H4: Bistable genetic circuit hypothesis"

### Interactive Demo Concept
"Try Similarity Search"
- Input field for a sample hypothesis
- Shows mock results with similarity breakdown
- "This would find 3 related hypotheses in your history"

## Animations

### Search Results
- Results fade in one by one (staggered)
- Similarity scores count up
- Match highlights pulse briefly

### What-If
- Confidence bar animating between states
- "If supports" and "If challenges" branches animate
- Recommendation appears with subtle glow

### Robustness
- Meter fills from left to right
- Evidence icons stack up under hypothesis
- Color shifts from red (fragile) to green (robust)

### Anomaly
- Anomaly card has subtle "glitch" animation
- Connection lines to conflicting hypotheses
- "Spawned" arrow animation to new hypothesis

## Desktop vs Mobile

### Desktop
- Feature cards in horizontal scroll or grid
- Interactive demos inline
- Side-by-side before/after visualizations

### Mobile
- Vertical stack with expandable details
- Simplified interactive elements
- Swipe to see different scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:53:33.487801561-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:31:14.391786654-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T00:31:14.391786654-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:53:33.520578125-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:38.966934611-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.6","title":"Feature: Operator Framework Section","description":"# Operator Framework Section

## ⚠️ RISK ASSESSMENT

**This section may alienate non-academic visitors.**

The mathematical notation (⊘, ✂, ⟂, ⊞) and abstract composition formula are intellectually exciting for the right audience, but may:
- Overwhelm casual visitors
- Create a "this isn't for me" feeling
- Break the flow of the page narrative

**Mitigations:**
1. **Position**: Place near the end of the page (position 7 of 8), after trust has been established
2. **Framing**: Label as "Deep Dive" or "For the Curious" to set expectations
3. **Progressive disclosure**: Default to collapsed/minimal, expand on click
4. **Alternative entry**: Offer a simpler "plain English" version alongside the notation
5. **Exit ramp**: Ensure users can skip directly to "Get Started" if overwhelmed

**Decision needed during implementation**: Consider whether this section should be:
- A full section on the landing page (current plan)
- An expandable subsection within another feature
- A linked separate page for deep dives

## Purpose
Show the cognitive operators that encode Brenner's methodology. This is intellectually exciting - showing how abstract thinking patterns become composable, executable functions.

## Key Messages
1. Brenner's methods encoded as operators
2. Operators are composable into pipelines
3. Each operator has templates to get started
4. Mathematical notation makes the method precise

## Content Structure

### Section Title
"The Operator Algebra: Brenner's Methods as Executable Code"

### Introduction
"Sydney Brenner's breakthrough wasn't just his discoveries - it was his method. We've encoded his cognitive patterns as composable operators that you can apply systematically."

### Plain English Version (NEW - offer first)
Before the mathematical notation, offer a simpler framing:

> **The Brenner Method in 4 Steps:**
> 1. **Split the levels** - Separate the "what" from the "how"
> 2. **Design killing tests** - Find experiments that eliminate possibilities
> 3. **Choose your system** - Pick the easiest organism/model to test with
> 4. **Check the physics** - Make sure it's physically possible

*"Want the precise notation? Expand below."*

### Core Operators Display

#### ⊘ Level-Split
**"Separate program from interpreter"**
- Message vs machine, genotype vs phenotype
- Includes "chastity vs impotence" diagnostic
- Template: "What is the information? What is the mechanism?"

Visual: Split diagram showing two levels with examples

#### ✂ Exclusion-Test  
**"Design tests that eliminate, not confirm"**
- Forbidden patterns: what cannot occur if H is true
- Discriminative power rating (1-5)
- Template: "If H1 is true, we should NEVER see..."

Visual: Hypothesis tree with branches being pruned

#### ⟂ Object-Transpose
**"Change the system until the test is easy"**
- Choose organism/model strategically
- "The experimental object is a design variable"
- Template: "What system would make this test cheap and unambiguous?"

Visual: Multiple organisms/systems with selection arrow

#### ⊞ Scale-Check
**"Stay imprisoned in physics"**
- Validate against physical constraints
- Calculate timescales, length scales, energy scales
- Template: "Is this physically possible at the relevant scale?"

Visual: Scale bars with constraint ranges

### The Core Composition (Expandable by default on desktop, collapsed on mobile)
"The signature Brenner move expressed as operator composition:"

```
(⌂ ∘ ✂ ∘ ≡ ∘ ⊘) powered by (↑ ∘ ⟂ ∘ 🔧) constrained by (⊞) kept honest by (ΔE ∘ †)
```

Expanded explanation:
- Start from a paradox (◊)
- Split levels (⊘)
- Extract invariants (≡)
- Design exclusion tests (✂)
- Materialize as decision procedure (⌂)
- Power by amplification (↑) in well-chosen system (⟂) you build yourself (🔧)
- Constrain by physics (⊞)
- Keep honest with exception handling (ΔE) and theory killing (†)

### Extended Operators (collapsed by default)
- ↑ Amplify: Use selection, dominance, regime switches
- ◊ Paradox-hunt: Use contradictions as beacons
- ⊕ Cross-domain: Import tools from other fields
- ∿ Dephase: Work out of phase with fashion
- † Theory-kill: Drop hypotheses when the world says no
- ⌂ Materialize: "What would I see if this were true?"

### Code Example
```typescript
import { pipe } from "@/lib/brenner-loop/operators/framework";

const brennerPipeline = pipe(
  levelSplit,        // Separate levels
  invariantExtract,  // Find what survives
  exclusionTest,     // Design killing experiments
  materialize,       // Compile to decision procedure
);

const result = brennerPipeline(hypothesis, context);
```

## Animations

### Operator Cards
- Each operator symbol has subtle glow/pulse
- Hovering shows expanded description
- Click animates to full explanation

### Composition Formula
- Operators appear one by one as user scrolls
- Connection symbols (∘) draw between them
- Final formula assembles with satisfying animation

### Pipeline Demo
- Code block with syntax highlighting
- Function calls highlight in sequence
- Arrow shows data flowing through pipeline

## Desktop vs Mobile

### Desktop
- Four core operators in 2x2 grid with large symbols
- Composition formula as prominent display
- Code example with live syntax highlighting
- Extended operators in expandable section

### Mobile
- **Start with plain English version**
- Vertical stack of operator cards
- Swipe to reveal each operator
- Composition formula COLLAPSED by default (click to expand)
- Code example in scrollable container","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:04.499978551-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:32:59.214396505-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T00:32:59.214396505-05:00","close_reason":"Added full Operator Framework Section with plain English version, 4 core operators, composition formula, 6 extended operators, and code example","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:04.53123185-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:40.041230553-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.7","title":"Feature: Technical Architecture Section","description":"# Technical Architecture Section

## Purpose
Build trust with technical users by showing the solid engineering foundation. Highlight the CLI-first design, offline capabilities, and security model.

## Key Messages
1. CLI-first: No API keys in code, use your own subscriptions
2. Local-first: Works offline, syncs when connected
3. Deterministic: Same inputs always produce same outputs
4. Auditable: Every action tracked and reversible

## Content Structure

### Section Title
"Built for Serious Work: Architecture That Respects Your Research"

### Key Architecture Points

#### CLI-First Design
**"Your Terminal, Your Subscriptions, Your Control"**

"Brenner Lab doesn't call AI APIs from code. Instead, CLI tools run in your terminal under your existing subscriptions (Claude Max, GPT Pro, Gemini Ultra). You see everything, control everything."

Benefits:
- No API keys to manage or leak
- No rate limits beyond your subscription
- Full session context preserved
- Real-time visibility into agent work

Visual: Terminal with agents running in tmux panes

#### Local-First Storage
**"Your Data Stays With You"**

"Hypotheses, evidence, and session artifacts are stored locally. The system works offline and syncs when you're ready."

Components:
- IndexedDB for structured data
- localStorage for queue state
- File locks for concurrent access
- Background sync when online

Visual: Data flow diagram showing local storage → optional cloud sync

#### Deterministic Merging
**"Reproducible by Design"**

"When multiple agents produce outputs, they merge deterministically. Two runs with the same inputs produce identical outputs."

Merge rules:
- Timestamp ordering
- Last-write-wins for conflicts
- Killed items preserved in history
- Conflicts logged for audit

Visual: Merge diagram showing deltas combining

#### Security Model
**"Fail-Closed, Not Fail-Open"**

"Lab Mode is protected by default. Unauthorized requests get 404 (no information leakage), not 401."

Layers:
1. Environment check (BRENNER_LAB_MODE)
2. Authentication (Cloudflare Access or lab secret)
3. Timing-safe secret comparison
4. Content policy enforcement

### The Join-Key Contract
"Thread ID is the universal join key that ties everything together:"

```
Thread ID ↔ Agent Mail thread
         ↔ ntm session
         ↔ Artifact file path
         ↔ Beads issue ID
```

Visual: Diagram showing thread ID connecting all components

### CLI Command Showcase
Selected commands with brief descriptions:

```bash
# Search the corpus
brenner corpus search "exclusion test"

# Start a session
brenner session start --thread-id RS-20251230-cell-fate \
  --question "How do cells determine position?"

# Check session status
brenner session status --thread-id RS-20251230-cell-fate --watch

# Compile artifacts
brenner session compile --thread-id RS-20251230-cell-fate
```

### Performance Stats
- CLI startup: < 50ms
- Artifact compilation: < 50ms
- Test suite: 4300+ tests, < 30s
- Page load: < 2s target

## Animations

### Architecture Diagram
- Components fade in as user scrolls
- Connection lines draw between components
- Data flow animated as moving dots

### Terminal Demo
- Typing animation for commands
- Output appearing line by line
- Cursor blinking

### Security Layers
- Layers stack up visually
- Each layer highlights when described
- "Protected" badge appears at end

## Desktop vs Mobile

### Desktop
- Large architecture diagram with hover states
- Side-by-side code examples
- Performance stats as animated counters

### Mobile
- Simplified architecture diagram
- Collapsible code examples
- Stats as horizontal progress bars","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PearlBarn","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:29.338015535-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:35:47.687200687-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T00:35:47.687200687-05:00","close_reason":"Created TechnicalArchitectureSection component with CLI-First Design, Local-First Storage, Deterministic Merging, Security Model, Join-Key Contract, CLI Command Showcase, and Performance Stats. 7 tests pass, 0 lint errors.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:29.368731833-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:41.755406433-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","title":"Feature: Get Started & CTA Section","description":"# Get Started & CTA Section

## Purpose
Convert interest into action. This section should make getting started feel easy and exciting, with clear next steps for different user types.

## Key Messages
1. Installation is one command
2. Multiple paths based on your goals
3. Community and support available
4. 100% free and open source

## Content Structure

### Section Title
"Ready to Transform Your Research?"

### Installation Block
**"One Command to Get Started"**

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/brenner_bot/main/install.sh | bash
```

Options badges:
- `--easy-mode` - Minimal prompts
- `--verify` - Checksum verification
- `--system` - System-wide install

Alternative: "Or install from source →"

### User Paths
Three paths based on user intent:

#### For Researchers
"Run your first Brenner session"
1. Install the CLI
2. Build an excerpt from the corpus
3. Start a session with your research question
4. Review the compiled artifact

CTA: "Start Researching →"

#### For Developers
"Contribute to the project"
1. Clone the repository
2. Run the test suite (4300+ tests)
3. Check the Beads roadmap
4. Submit a PR

CTA: "View on GitHub →"

#### For the Curious
"Understand the method first"
1. Read the Brenner method overview
2. Explore the transcripts
3. Try the jargon dictionary
4. Watch the demo (if available)

CTA: "Learn the Method →"

### Quick Start Preview
Show first session in 4 steps:

```bash
# 1. Verify installation
brenner doctor --skip-ntm --skip-cass --skip-cm

# 2. Search the corpus
brenner corpus search "discriminative test"

# 3. Build an excerpt
brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161 > excerpt.md

# 4. Start a session
brenner session start \
  --thread-id RS-$(date +%Y%m%d)-first \
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \
  --question "What makes a good discriminative test?"
```

### Social Proof Row
- GitHub stars badge (live)
- Test count badge (4300+ passing)
- License badge (MIT)
- "Built with ❤️ for science"

### Community Links
- GitHub Discussions
- Issue Tracker
- Contributing Guide
- Discord/Slack (if applicable)

### Final CTA
Large, prominent button: "Get Started Now"
Secondary: "Read the Docs" | "View Source"

### Footer Note
"Brenner Bot is 100% free and open source. No hidden costs, no data collection, no vendor lock-in. Just rigorous research tools."

## Animations

### Installation Block
- Code block has copy button with success animation
- Typing cursor in command
- "Copied!" tooltip on click

### User Path Cards
- Cards have subtle lift on hover
- Arrow CTAs pulse gently
- Badges animate in on scroll

### Quick Start Steps
- Steps reveal one by one
- Code syntax highlights progressively
- Success checkmarks appear

### Final CTA
- Button has gradient animation on hover
- Subtle glow/pulse to draw attention
- Satisfying click animation

## Desktop vs Mobile

### Desktop
- Three user path cards side by side
- Quick start as full-width code block
- Final CTA centered with generous padding

### Mobile
- User paths as vertical accordion
- Quick start as horizontal scrollable
- Sticky CTA at bottom of viewport
- Simplified social proof row","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:53.018598018-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:38:20.608935118-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:54:53.049769022-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:49.800334723-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:49.834218966-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:49.86142739-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:51.159592456-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:51.194537776-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:58:51.225024753-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.9","title":"Feature: Animation & Scroll Effects System","description":"# Animation & Scroll Effects System

## Purpose
Create a cohesive animation system that enhances understanding without overwhelming. Animations should feel purposeful, performant, and delightful.

## Key Principles

### 1. Purpose Over Polish
Every animation should serve a purpose:
- Guide attention to important elements
- Show relationships between concepts
- Provide feedback for interactions
- Create smooth transitions between states

### 2. Performance First
- Use CSS transforms and opacity (GPU-accelerated)
- Avoid animating layout properties (width, height, margin)
- Use will-change sparingly and remove after animation
- Target 60fps on mid-range devices

### 3. Accessibility
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Provide static fallbacks
- Don't hide content behind animations
- Ensure animations don't trigger vestibular issues

## Animation Inventory

### Entry Animations (on scroll into view)

#### Fade Up
- Elements fade in while moving up 20-30px
- Duration: 400-600ms
- Easing: ease-out
- Use for: Text blocks, cards, images

#### Stagger Reveal
- Multiple items animate in sequence
- Stagger delay: 50-100ms per item
- Use for: Lists, grids, feature cards

#### Scale In
- Element scales from 0.95 to 1 with fade
- Duration: 300-400ms
- Use for: Buttons, badges, icons

#### Draw In
- SVG paths draw themselves
- Duration: 800-1200ms
- Use for: Diagrams, connection lines, icons

### Scroll-Linked Animations

#### Parallax
- Background elements move slower than foreground
- Factor: 0.3-0.5x scroll speed
- Use for: Hero backgrounds, decorative elements

#### Progress-Linked
- Element state tied to scroll progress
- Example: Timeline fills as user scrolls
- Use for: Timelines, progress indicators

#### Reveal on Threshold
- Elements animate when 20-30% visible
- Use IntersectionObserver for performance
- Use for: All content sections

### Micro-Interactions

#### Button Hover
- Scale to 1.02-1.05
- Subtle shadow increase
- Background color shift
- Duration: 150-200ms

#### Card Hover (Desktop)
- Lift effect (translateY: -4px)
- Shadow increase
- Optional 3D tilt (rotateX/Y based on cursor position)
- Duration: 200-300ms

#### Focus States
- Visible focus ring for keyboard navigation
- Smooth transition to focus state
- Consistent across all interactive elements

#### Click/Tap Feedback
- Quick scale down (0.97) then up (1.0)
- Duration: 100-150ms
- Audio feedback consideration (optional)

### Transition Animations

#### Section to Section
- Smooth scroll with easing
- Intersection-based content loading
- No jarring jumps

#### Modal/Panel Open
- Fade + scale from 0.95
- Backdrop blur (if supported)
- Duration: 200-300ms

#### Accordion Expand
- Height animation with overflow handling
- Content fades in after expand
- Duration: 300-400ms

## Implementation Details

### Framer Motion Configuration
```typescript
// Shared animation variants
export const fadeUp = {
  hidden: { opacity: 0, y: 20 },
  visible: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
};

export const stagger = {
  visible: {
    transition: {
      staggerChildren: 0.1,
    },
  },
};

// Scroll-triggered component
function AnimateOnScroll({ children }) {
  return (
    <motion.div
      initial="hidden"
      whileInView="visible"
      viewport={{ once: true, margin: "-100px" }}
      variants={fadeUp}
      transition={{ duration: 0.5, ease: "easeOut" }}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  );
}
```

### Reduced Motion Support
```typescript
// Hook for respecting user preference
function useReducedMotion() {
  return typeof window !== 'undefined' 
    ? window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches 
    : false;
}

// Usage
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
const animationDuration = prefersReducedMotion ? 0 : 0.5;
```

### Performance Monitoring
- Use Framer Motion's useReducedMotion
- Monitor frame rate in development
- Test on low-end devices
- Profile with Chrome DevTools

## Animation Timing Guidelines

| Animation Type | Duration | Easing |
|----------------|----------|--------|
| Micro-interaction | 100-200ms | ease-out |
| Content reveal | 400-600ms | ease-out |
| Complex diagram | 800-1200ms | ease-in-out |
| Page transition | 300-500ms | ease-in-out |
| Loading state | 1000ms+ | linear (for spinners) |

## Testing Checklist
- [ ] Works at 60fps on Chrome/Firefox/Safari
- [ ] Respects prefers-reduced-motion
- [ ] No layout shift during animations
- [ ] Keyboard focus visible during animations
- [ ] Touch targets remain accessible
- [ ] Animations don't block content access","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:55:22.321154701-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T00:17:49.410968182-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T00:17:49.410968182-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Framer Motion animation system: variants, hooks, and components with reduced-motion support. 42 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs.9","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-f8vs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-05T23:55:22.352589141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
